g.text: not yet supported (v1 = azure-container-apps only); stop
[ambiguity OR a GENERALIZED landmine (app-config, purpose, or access-path)]
SURFACE the gap, never bury it as a runbook STOP note
MANDATORY dialect confirmation, BEFORE any artifact (USE-DEPLOY-DIALECT)
name READER + WRITER for every secured store;
each needs an identity and an admitted network path
[read-but-never-written, OR a writer with no admitted path]
BLOCKING: SURFACE the hole + connectivity options
HAND OFF to SEQ-DEPLOY-EXECUTE (run the runbook)
Precondition: the PLAN phase produced diagram + IaC + runbook.
The runbook is now the EXECUTION PLAN the skill walks itself.
BLOCKING: surface the subscription mismatch; STOP
validate IaC (terraform validate / az bicep build)
EXECUTE — autonomous except irreversible; HALT on first error (OCL-DEPLOY-EXEC-HALT-ON-ERROR)
resource group + ACR create; docker build + push (idempotent, AUTONOMOUS)
(one gate covering infra apply + secret writes + migration job)
apply infra (terraform apply / az deployment create)
provision Container Apps, ACR, Postgres, Key Vault, OpenAI
deploy new revision at 0% traffic; health-check
HALT — report the failing step; run NO further step
SURFACE full billing multi-factor gate state
PUBLIC_HOST, APPLE_ENVIRONMENT, App Attest prod); BLOCKING confirm
route 100% traffic to the new revision (GO LIVE)
report — deployed and LIVE, or HALTED at the failing step
New revision deploys at 0% traffic and is health-checked
BEFORE the go-live cutover, so a bad rollout cannot take
down the running revision. The billing gate is never
auto-flipped — GO-LIVE is the one stop full-auto never skips.
